cpu: AMD Ryzen 7 2700U with Radeon Vega Mobile Gfx
notes: lowered all settings to minimum other then textures to keep the frame rate more stable turned off vsync enabled sound and played about a hour with the cam zoomed all the way out.
title: Dragon Age: Origins - Ultimate Edition

notes: The game does not start if Antialiasing is set to 8x. I didn't try 4x, but 2x works just fine. The game also starts with sound disabled, but a quick trip to the settings fixes that. I haven't encountered any other issues, and it runs at 100+ FPS.
title: Dragon Age: Origins - Ultimate Edition

notes: Just ensure that when you get the splash screen, you go to configure, make sure that resolution is set correctly, turn off AA and make sure that sound is on since it's off by default.  Mods work, however you cannot use the DAupdater, you'll have to manually add them.
title: Dragon Age: Origins - Ultimate Edition

notes:
Runs well, but has a few issues.
1) Camera mouse movement is glitchy after a while, like rotating much faster than normal or rotating in wrong direction
2) When tabbing out of game and entering again, sometimes the game becomes unresponsive to mouse/keyboard input. Only restart game helps.
title: Dragon Age: Origins - Ultimate Edition

notes:
Regularly crashed after a while, probably due to going over 2GB RAM - fixed it quickly by adding "PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 %command%" into the steam launch options.

There's also a mouse issue where looking around causes irregular movement - turning off Fullscreen fixed this issue.
title: Dragon Age: Origins - Ultimate Edition

notes: The game run great on 3.7-8. I couldn't run it on the latest patch. It has very bad screen tearing with V-sync on, so you should leave it off. Otherwise, same performance as Windows.
title: Dragon Age: Origins - Ultimate Edition

notes: You'll have the settings menu in the launcher to configure the screen and sound settings, because doing that in-game gives issues. Other then that, I've played all the way through to Ostagar without any issues. Also, online functionality seems to be broken, although that might be on EA, not Proton.
title: Dragon Age: Origins - Ultimate Edition

notes: I haven't run into any issues so far. I've been able to connect to the online services and synchronize the achievements.
title: Dragon Age: Origins - Ultimate Edition

notes: Some textures were missing with AA at 8x, but they worked fine with AA turned off. I didn't try anything in between. If you have any oddness with textures, try turning off AA.
title: Dragon Age: Origins - Ultimate Edition

notes: Mod installation doesn't work properly because of the way how Steam symlinks your user account with a generic steamuser account. If you want to use mods, and the Nexus Mod Organizer, then run Dragon Age in a traditional Wine prefix using Q4Wine
title: Dragon Age: Origins - Ultimate Edition

notes: Didn't try any tweaks, the game would just straight up not refresh properly when trying to complete any sort of character customization action and randomly when in the middle of a conversation and during movement and it started working properly again while using shift+tab. I didn't even bother to try it during combat.
title: Dragon Age: Origins - Ultimate Edition

notes:
Set antialiasing to 2x as some other reports noted issues with higher settings. No problems adjusting sound & graphics settings in-game, but adjusting them in the configure options from the launcher didn't work as hardware settings weren't properly detected.

Crashed once, graphics slightly blurry, and trying to move the camera with right click + drag makes the camera go haywire even if the sensitivity is turned down. Also if the launcher is closed without launching the game, the process hangs and might need to be force quit. Haven't noticed any serious issues with gameplay, however.

Some luck installing dazip-based mods by renaming DAUpdater.exe in the bin_ship folder to DAOrigins.exe, running the game from the launcher to open the updater, installing the dazip file, and then restoring the original .exe filenames. Not extensively tested with mods.
title: Dragon Age: Origins - Ultimate Edition

cpu: AMD A6-4400M APU with Radeon HD Graphics
notes: Seemed to crash at character creation.  Pressed Alt+Tab to try and window out.  Accidentally discovered that alt tab progresses the cut scenes, menu fades, and dialogue conversations.  So I played continuously through until my first available exit point (alt tabbing about 200 times).  Main menu runs flawlessly, as it were.
title: Dragon Age: Origins - Ultimate Edition
